title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — June 6, 2026
headline: Bolivia's CODIFIED-IMPUNITY-ESCALATION crosses Developing to Critical in the pipeline's second band crossing in four days: President Paz signed a presumption-of-legality military-force law, 10 protesters killed and 365 arrested, superseding the June 5 proposal at a materially larger −10.0 magnitude; Venezuela confirmed at 18.0 as UN FFM crimes-against-humanity findings document already-priced harm amid a partial de-escalation; Haiti confirmed at near-floor 4.7 on record 1.5 million displaced, gang-driven.
summary: One change proposal this cycle. Bolivia (28.4→18.4, −10.0, Developing→Critical) is the only scored movement and a band crossing: President Paz signed a law (June 8, 2026) authorizing military force against protesters with a presumption-of-legality impunity shield — a codified structural state-conduct change that degrades ACC, INT, BND, EQU, and EMP beyond the attested casualties already documented on June 5. The toll stands at 10 killed, 37 injured, and 365 arrested; 25 union leaders including COB chief Mario Argullo face terrorism charges; La Paz and El Alto are near-fully blockaded. This proposal supersedes the June 5 Bolivia proposal (28.4→22.8, −5.6, remained Developing) at a materially larger magnitude and crosses the Developing/Critical boundary. Venezuela confirmed at 18.0: the HRW universal-jurisdiction report and UN FFM crimes-against-humanity findings document a decade-plus pattern already encoded in the Critical composite, coinciding with a partial de-escalation (political-prisoner releases post-Maduro removal) — external accountability milestones document past harm rather than revealing new un-priced conduct. Haiti confirmed at near-floor 4.7: IOM's record 1.5 million displaced are gang-driven amid a near-collapsed state; the quantitative deepening of a priced incapacity does not produce a new proposal. Five floor confirmations (Russia, Myanmar, Sudan, Yemen, Israel). Two boundary cases carry forward: Uganda at 20.3 (0.3 points above Critical; Ebola PHEIC with Kampala spread) and Oracle at 20.6 (0.6 points above Critical; WARN Act June 15 deadline nine days out). Two open band-crossing proposals now pending simultaneously: Bolivia 06-06 (28.4→18.4) and United States 06-04 (23.4→17.5).

topSignals.title: Bolivia 28.4 → 18.4 (−10.0) — CODIFIED-IMPUNITY-ESCALATION: Signed Military-Force Law with Presumption-of-Legality Shield Crosses Developing to Critical
topSignals.description: President Arce Paz signed into law (June 8, 2026) legislation authorizing the military to use force against protesters and granting soldiers a presumption-of-legality shield — their actions deemed lawful unless proven otherwise. This codified impunity structure is a durable state-conduct change that pre-commits the apparatus to non-acknowledgment of harm, pulling ACC to 1.3 and INT to 1.5 and driving the entity across the Developing/Critical boundary. The toll stands at 10 killed, 37 injured, and 365 arrested (independent public-ombudsman); 25 union leaders including COB executive secretary Mario Argullo face terrorism charges; La Paz and El Alto are near-fully blockaded amid Bolivia's worst economic crisis in four decades. This proposal supersedes the June 5 Bolivia proposal (28.4→22.8, −5.6, remained Developing): the signed-into-law impunity apparatus is materially distinct from and more severe than incident-level casualties alone. Dimension moves: ACC 1.8→1.3, INT 2.0→1.5, BND 2.3→1.7, EQU 1.9→1.5, EMP 2.4→1.8, ACT 1.9→1.7, AWR 2.4→2.2, SYS 2.4→2.2. Confidence medium-high: terrorism charges remain filed-not-adjudicated; the magnitude rests on the signed law plus attested deaths and detentions, not charge outcomes.

topSignals.title: Venezuela 18.0 — UN FFM Crimes-Against-Humanity Findings Document Already-Priced Harm Amid Partial De-escalation
topSignals.description: HRW's June 2 universal-jurisdiction report and the UN Fact-Finding Mission's confirmed crimes-against-humanity findings (December 2025) — covering GNB extrajudicial killings, enforced disappearances, and torture, with 884 political prisoners as of November 2025 — document a decade-plus pattern already encoded in Venezuela's Critical 18.0 composite. Critically, the post-Maduro removal trajectory (January 2026) has produced a partial de-escalation: the interim government has begun releasing political prisoners, a development the FFM itself characterized as positive. External accountability milestones document past harm; they are not new un-priced state conduct. Venezuela confirms at 18.0. The score could move in either direction: a renewed mass-detention wave or ICC warrant would constitute a new scored event; a sustained and verified prisoner-release trajectory is the first improvement signal.

topSignals.title: Haiti 4.7 — IOM Records 1.5 Million Displaced; Gang-Driven Displacement Encoded by Near-Floor Incapacity
topSignals.description: IOM reported (June 5) that Haiti's displaced population reached a record 1.5 million — a majority women and girls — with Port-au-Prince IDPs exceeding 300,000 for the first time and more than 110,000 forcibly returned since January. The dominant driver is gang violence amid a near-collapsed state. Haiti's near-floor 4.7 encodes profound state incapacity across EQU, BND, ACC, SYS, and INT (all at or near the 1.0 minimum): the June figures represent a quantitative deepening of a priced collapse, not new state-attributable harm. The designation holds absent documented state or MSS-force complicity in civilian harm, which would be the conversion trigger toward the absolute floor.

topSignals.title: Uganda 20.3 — Ebola Bundibugyo Spreads to Kampala; Boundary at 0.3 Points Above Critical
topSignals.description: Uganda's cross-border Ebola Bundibugyo spread stands at 19 cases and 2 deaths; 8 of 9 new cases are in Kampala (population 3.9 million), with 5 from local transmission under an active WHO PHEIC. The outbreak is an exogenous epidemiological shock revealing Uganda's already-priced state-response capacity rather than documenting new deliberate state conduct. Uganda confirms at 20.3 — 0.3 points above the Critical boundary — under the NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY-NON-DOUBLE-COUNT ruling. The conversion trigger is documented deliberate state obstruction of the WHO response, including access denial, fund diversion, or case-data suppression. Uganda (rank 149, composite 20.3) is distinct from the Democratic Republic of Congo (rank 181, composite 2.3) and the Republic of Congo (rank 148, composite 20.3).

topSignals.title: United States 23.4 — ICE Rescinds Post-Release Death-Reporting Requirement; 18 Detention Deaths YTD; Open 06-04 Proposal Reinforced
topSignals.description: ICE rescinded the 30-day post-release death-reporting requirement — the 18th detention death of 2026 at approximately one every six days, the highest rate in more than two decades, deepens the transparency-degradation pattern within the same conduct layer priced by the open 06-04 proposal (23.4→17.5, Developing→Critical). This is same-regime continuation that reinforces without altering the −5.9 magnitude. No duplicate proposal was created. The proposed 17.5 Critical composite remains the open assessment record; the appellate review of the Murphy (D. Mass.) and Cobb (D.D.C.) deportation injunctions is the primary reassessment signal.

topSignals.title: Sudan 0.0 — UAE Documented Arming the RSF; El Fasher Genocide Hallmarks Confirmed by UN
topSignals.description: UAE documented arms supply to the Rapid Support Forces (June 8) constitutes a new active-perpetration-reinforcement signal at the absolute floor. The UN fact-finding commission has confirmed 6,000-plus killings in El Fasher with hallmarks of genocide against the Zaghawa and Fur populations. Sudan's fourth year of civil war sustains 28 million food-insecure and 11.8 million displaced. The absolute 0.0 floor holds — no score movement is possible at this level, but the external-actor-complicity pattern (UAE arms supply to a force the UN has documented committing genocide) deepens the evidentiary record.

topSignals.title: Oracle Corporation 20.6 — WARN Act Deadline Nine Days Out; Boundary at 0.6 Points Above Critical
topSignals.description: Oracle's WARN Act sign-or-forfeit window for approximately 30,000 affected workers closes June 15 — nine days from this cycle. The remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory is the conversion mechanism from the 0.6-point-above-Critical baseline. The Washington state class action and New Jersey state WARN 90-day proceedings are the two active adjudication channels. Oracle's June 15 deadline is the most proximate known hard trigger date across all assessed entities this cycle. Any adjudicated or corroborated finding on the avoidance theory before or after June 15 constitutes an immediate scoring trigger.

topSignals.title: India 15.6 — ~5,000 Bengali Muslim Deportees; Confirmed Citizens Expelled Without Verification; UN Condemns
topSignals.description: The expulsion of Bengali-speaking Muslims to Bangladesh has escalated from an estimated 1,500 to approximately 5,000, with HRW documenting confirmed Indian citizens expelled without verification, abuse at gunpoint, and 145 reportedly disappeared. The June 6 escalation deepens the EQU 1.3, EMP 1.4, ACC 1.4 profile established by the applied June 1 downgrade (22.7→15.6); it reinforces rather than reveals new un-priced harm and does not generate a second proposal. The 145 reported disappearances are a forward watch item: if confirmed as state-attributable enforced disappearances at scale, further scored movement beyond 15.6 becomes warranted.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Judicial annulment or legislative repeal of the June 8 presumption-of-legality military-force law is the primary reversal signal. Further military deployments with casualties under the signed law reinforce the proposed 18.4 Critical baseline. Charge adjudication for the 25 union leaders is a secondary directional indicator.
boundaryWatch.note: Bolivia crossed from Developing to Critical on the signed codified-impunity law plus attested 10 killed and 365 arrested. The proposed composite of 18.4 sits 1.6 points above the floor of the Critical band. The June 5 proposal (28.4→22.8) is superseded by this larger-magnitude finding.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Appellate review of the Murphy (D. Mass.) and Cobb (D.D.C.) deportation injunctions is the primary reassessment signal. Reinstatement of the rescinded 30-day post-release death-reporting requirement would be the first upward-direction indicator within the open proposal.
boundaryWatch.note: United States sits 3.4 points below the Developing/Critical boundary at the proposed 17.5 composite. The 06-04 band-crossing proposal rests on Tier-5 adjudicated judicial findings that the core deportation architecture is unlawful. The June 6 ICE death-reporting rescission deepens the documented conduct pattern without altering the proposed magnitude.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Documented deliberate state obstruction of the WHO Ebola response — access denial, fund diversion, or case-data suppression — converts the exogenous-overlay hold to a scored crossing into Critical from the 0.3-point-above-Critical baseline.
boundaryWatch.note: Uganda sits 0.3 points above Critical — the narrowest active country margin this cycle. Ebola Bundibugyo spread has reached Kampala (8 of 9 new cases) under a WHO PHEIC; the exogenous epidemiological shock reveals priced state-response capacity absent documented deliberate obstruction.

boundaryWatch.trigger: WARN Act last-working-day window closes June 15 — nine days from this cycle. NJ state WARN 90-day proceedings and the Washington class action on the remote-reclassification avoidance theory are the active adjudication channels.
boundaryWatch.note: Oracle at 20.6 is 0.6 points above Critical. The June 15 WARN deadline is the most proximate known hard trigger date across all assessed entities this cycle. Adverse WARN-avoidance adjudication would likely produce a scored crossing into Critical from the current baseline.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Adverse adjudication or settlement of the Australia $1.43B PFAS claim or the Minnesota MPCA PFOS suit, or rejection of 3M's military-contractor federal immunity defense in the Minnesota proceeding, converts the sub-threshold modeled downgrade to a scored proposal into Critical.
boundaryWatch.note: 3M sits 0.3 points above Critical — the narrowest active corporate margin. Australia and Minnesota MPCA sovereign PFAS suits carry forward from June 4. Prior settled reparation ($850M Minnesota 2018; A$408M Australian community) holds ACC above the floor under the FILED-BUT-UNADJUDICATED plus ADJUDICATED-PRIOR-REPARATION framing.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Documented deliberate state obstruction of humanitarian access, a state-attributable mass-casualty event, or confirmation of famine areas convert the PRICED-CONTINUATION carry-forward to a scorable downgrade.
boundaryWatch.note: Nigeria at 21.9 is 1.9 points above Critical. Approximately 34.7 million lean-season food-insecure and 3.5 million IDPs reflect non-state armed group and structural-fragility drivers already priced at the 21.9 baseline.

emergingRisks.risk: Bolivia's signed presumption-of-legality military-force law represents a codified impunity structure that pre-commits the apparatus to non-acknowledgment of harm; the proposed 18.4 Critical baseline rests on the law remaining in force.
emergingRisks.detail: The June 8 signed law distinguishes this cycle's Bolivia finding from incident-level casualty analysis. The law is a durable state-conduct change: so long as it remains in force, the ACC 1.3 and INT 1.5 anchors reflect a structural commitment to impunity rather than a situational repression pattern. Judicial annulment or legislative repeal is the primary reversal signal. Further military deployments with casualties under the law's cover would reinforce the 18.4 floor and potentially warrant additional dimension moves.

emergingRisks.risk: Two open band-crossing proposals (Bolivia 06-06 at 28.4→18.4 and United States 06-04 at 23.4→17.5) are now accumulating corroborating evidence simultaneously — the highest simultaneous band-crossing-proposal count in the current cycle sequence.
emergingRisks.detail: Bolivia's 06-06 proposal supersedes the 06-05 proposal at a materially larger magnitude; the US 06-04 proposal is reinforced each cycle by same-regime-continuation evidence. Both are Developing-to-Critical band crossings with medium-high confidence. Both have the proposed composite as the lower-confidence reading and the accumulating evidence record as the higher-confidence signal.

emergingRisks.risk: Ebola Bundibugyo has reached Kampala with 8 of 9 new Uganda cases in the capital; Uganda sits 0.3 points above Critical at the boundary, with documented deliberate state obstruction as the conversion trigger.
emergingRisks.detail: Uganda's Kampala-cluster transmission pattern (5 from local transmission; population 3.9 million) represents a qualitative escalation from rural cross-border spread. The DRC source outbreak at 515 cases and 91 deaths continues with 20% contact-tracing coverage and no approved Bundibugyo vaccine. The CDC worst-case model of 20,000 cases is the operative planning scenario. The exogenous-overlay hold rests entirely on the absence of documented deliberate state conduct; any confirmed access denial, fund diversion, or WHO-response obstruction converts both entities.

emergingRisks.risk: Oracle's WARN Act sign-or-forfeit window closes June 15 — the most proximate hard trigger date across all assessed entities — with the remote-reclassification avoidance theory carrying 0.6 points of boundary buffer.
emergingRisks.detail: Approximately 30,000 workers are in Oracle's WARN window. The remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory is the conversion mechanism from the 0.6-point-above-Critical baseline. NJ state WARN 90-day proceedings and the Washington class action are both active. Any adjudicated or corroborated finding on the avoidance theory before or after June 15 constitutes an immediate scoring trigger from the current baseline.

emergingRisks.risk: India's 145 reported disappearances among approximately 5,000 deportees document a potential new harm category — state-attributable enforced disappearances — beyond the priced refoulement and expulsion pattern already encoded at 15.6.
emergingRisks.detail: The applied June 1 downgrade (22.7→15.6) prices the discriminatory mass-expulsion pattern. The 145 reportedly disappeared are not yet confirmed as state-attributable enforced disappearances; if confirmed at scale, this constitutes a new harm category beyond the priced EQU 1.3/EMP 1.4/ACC 1.4 profile and would be independently scorable. The EU Returns Regulation enters force June 12, adding a structural enforcement layer relevant to India's non-refoulement posture.

emergingRisks.risk: Sudan's active-perpetration floor is deepened by the UAE's documented arms supply to the RSF — an external-actor-complicity pattern that reinforces the conflict-driven genocide evidence without producing a new proposal from the absolute floor.
emergingRisks.detail: UAE documented arms supply to a force the UN has confirmed committed genocide in El Fasher represents a new active-perpetration-reinforcement signal. The absolute 0.0 floor cannot move lower; the significance is evidentiary and forward-looking — the documented complicity pattern creates a new category of accountability evidence that may be consequential in future international-law proceedings and is logged as a deepening of the floor rationale.

emergingRisks.risk: EU AI Act GPAI transparency and systematic-risk documentation obligations take full effect August 2, 57 days from this cycle, with no assessed AI lab having published compliance documentation.
emergingRisks.detail: Non-compliance fines reach 35 million euros or 7% of global annual turnover. The compliance gap is structural across the assessed AI labs cluster. The August 2 date accumulates scoring weight as each cycle passes without disclosed compliance documentation. OpenAI's comprehensive full assessment remains overdue.

emergingRisks.risk: Venezuela's partial de-escalation trajectory (post-Maduro prisoner releases) has not yet crossed the threshold to a scored improvement; a reversal of the release trajectory or an ICC warrant issuance would each constitute a new scored event.
emergingRisks.detail: The UN FFM characterized the prisoner-release trajectory as positive. Sustaining and expanding releases to the full 884 documented political prisoners, accompanied by verified judicial reform, constitutes the improvement-signal pathway from the 18.0 Critical baseline. Conversely, a new mass-detention wave following the release pattern would be a newly scorable harm category, as the de-escalation trajectory would set a new before-and-after baseline.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Band Crossing: Bolivia Developing to Critical
sectorAlerts.headline: Bolivia's codified-impunity law crosses the Developing/Critical boundary at −10.0; the second band crossing in four pipeline days and the cycle's sole scored movement
observations: Bolivia: CODIFIED-IMPUNITY-ESCALATION — President Paz signed (June 8) a law authorizing military force against protesters with a presumption-of-legality shield; 10 killed, 365 arrested, 25 union leaders face terrorism charges including COB chief Argullo; La Paz and El Alto near-fully blockaded; 28.4→18.4, −10.0, Developing→Critical

This cycle's Bolivia ruling extends the 06-05 STATE-REPRESSION-IN-FLUX ruling by distinguishing a codified apparatus-level impunity structure (durable state-conduct change) from incident-level casualties; the codified structure is what drives the band crossing
The 06-05 Bolivia proposal (28.4→22.8, remained Developing) is superseded at the larger magnitude; the 06-05 file is left unmodified; the supersession is recorded in the 06-06 proposal

Two open band-crossing proposals now pending simultaneously: Bolivia 06-06 (Developing→Critical) and United States 06-04 (Developing→Critical); both are medium-high confidence with accumulating corroborating evidence each cycle

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Critical Band Confirmations and Methodology
sectorAlerts.headline: Venezuela confirmed at 18.0 on accountability-documentation-of-past-harm discipline; Haiti confirmed at near-floor 4.7 on non-state-driven displacement
observations: Venezuela at 18.0 Critical: HRW universal-jurisdiction report and UN FFM crimes-against-humanity findings document a decade-plus pattern already encoded in the composite; the partial de-escalation (prisoner releases post-Maduro removal) makes the framing sharper — the score could move in either direction depending on whether the release trajectory is sustained

Haiti at near-floor 4.7: record 1.5 million displaced (IOM June 5), majority women and girls, over 300,000 in Port-au-Prince; gang-driven displacement amid a near-collapsed state is encoded by the near-floor incapacity score, not active state-perpetration; converts toward the floor only on documented state or MSS complicity
India confirmed at 15.6 Critical: deportee count reached approximately 5,000; 145 reportedly disappeared logged as a forward watch item under the PRICED-IN-RECENT-ADJUSTMENT ruling (applied June 1)

sectorAlerts.headline: Five floor confirmations; Sudan deepened by UAE-documented RSF arms supply; UAE complicity in El Fasher genocide newly documented
observations: Sudan: UAE documented arming the RSF (June 8); UN confirms 6,000+ El Fasher killings with hallmarks of genocide against Zaghawa and Fur; 28 million food-insecure; 11.8 million displaced; active-perpetration floor deepened by external-actor-complicity finding
Russia: June 2 Kyiv attack killed 22 civilians including a 3-year-old and 8-year-old; Putin rejected peace talks June 6; 60,659 UN-verified civilian casualties since 2022
Myanmar: June 1 junta airstrikes on Kyaung Cha Twin IDP camp in Rakhine while residents slept; 2 killed, 5+ wounded; approximately 100 civilians killed in junta airstrikes since March 16

Israel: 856 Palestinians killed since October 2025 ceasefire; aid trucks reduced from 4,200 to 590 per week; 37-plus aid groups banned; no Gaza hospital fully operational; West Bank 36,000-plus displaced

Yemen: HRW and Amnesty issued joint demand June 7 for release of 73 detained UN and CSO staff; a WFP staffer died in Houthi detention; WFP terminated 365 Yemen staff contracts; 18.3 million food-insecure

sectorAlerts.headline: Oracle WARN deadline nine days out; UnitedHealth enforcement density sustained across four converging sovereign actions; health-insurer price-fixing cartel pattern documented
observations: Oracle: WARN Act sign-or-forfeit window closes June 15 — nine days from this cycle; approximately 30,000 workers; remote-reclassification avoidance theory pre-positioned for scored promotion; 0.6 points above Critical

UnitedHealth Group: DOJ criminal and civil MA-fraud probe, AZ AG June 1 cartel suit naming 8 insurers and MultiPlan, Lifepoint June 2 federal complaint, DOJ Antitrust MultiPlan grand-jury subpoena — sovereign-enforcement density at the highest concentration in the current assessment cycle

AZ AG cartel suit documents a health-insurer price-fixing pattern involving eight major insurers and MultiPlan; the cartel designation, if adjudicated, would materially reframe the healthcare sector's ACC and EQU profiles beyond UnitedHealth alone
3M at 20.3 (0.3 points above Critical): Australia $1.43B and Minnesota MPCA PFAS suits carry forward; Phase-2 MDL July 31 deadline is the next structural trigger

sectorAlerts.headline: Ebola Bundibugyo spreads to Kampala; Uganda sits at the Critical boundary with 8 of 9 new cases in the capital; DRC source outbreak at 515/91
observations: Uganda: 19 cases, 2 deaths; 8 of 9 new cases in Kampala (population 3.9 million), 5 from local transmission; WHO PHEIC active; 0.3 points above Critical — conversion trigger is documented deliberate state obstruction of the WHO response

DRC source outbreak: 515 cases, 91 deaths; contact tracing at 20% of known transmission chains; CDC worst-case scenario of 20,000 cases is the operative planning scenario; no approved Bundibugyo vaccine; 26.5 million concurrent food-insecure

The Kampala cluster is a qualitative escalation from rural cross-border spread: an urban Ebola outbreak in a capital of 3.9 million under 20% contact-tracing coverage and no approved vaccine represents the highest single outbreak risk in the current assessment cycle

sectorAlerts.headline: Bolivia's codified-impunity-escalation ruling distinguishes durable apparatus-level state conduct from incident-level casualties; extends the methodology applicable to Developing-band entities
observations: The CODIFIED-IMPUNITY-ESCALATION ruling (Bolivia anchor case) establishes that a signed-into-law presumption-of-legality impunity shield is categorically distinct from attested incident-level casualties: the law pre-commits the apparatus to non-acknowledgment of harm and contradicts any stated rights commitments at the structural level

This distinction drove the difference between the June 5 within-band proposal (−5.6, remained Developing) and the June 6 band-crossing proposal (−10.0, Developing→Critical): the same toll would not have crossed the boundary absent the codified apparatus change

The ruling applies across the Countries index: any Developing-band entity that enacts legislation providing an impunity shield for state actors using force against civilians is subject to the same structural scoring logic as Bolivia's June 8 law

Kenya (35.9 Developing, confirmed June 5) and other Developing-band entities with documented protest-crackdown patterns carry this ruling as a forward threshold: a signed impunity law, not merely force orders, is the conversion trigger to the band-crossing level

sectorAlerts.sector: ICE Detention and Transparency — US Immigration Enforcement
sectorAlerts.headline: ICE rescinds post-release death-reporting requirement; 18 detention deaths YTD reinforces open 06-04 proposal as same-regime continuation
observations: ICE's rescission of the 30-day post-release death-reporting requirement is an AB2/AB3 transparency-degradation signal that compounds the proposed ACC 1.4 floor in the open 06-04 proposal without altering the −5.9 magnitude

The 18th ICE in-custody death of 2026 — approximately one every six days — documents continuation of the record death-rate trajectory at the highest rate in two-plus decades; this is the deepest in-custody-death density in the current assessment cycle
The duplicate-suppression discipline held for the second consecutive US assessment: the June 6 evidence is same-regime continuation of the pattern priced in the 06-04 proposal
The proposed 17.5 Critical composite is the lower-confidence reading; the accumulating evidence record — including the June 6 transparency rollback — is the higher-confidence signal

sectorAlerts.headline: EU Returns Regulation enters force June 12; EU AI Act GPAI obligations take full effect August 2; both carry structured scoring triggers
observations: EU Returns Regulation enters force June 12 — six days from this cycle; India (15.6 Critical), Jordan (35.9 Developing), and Romania are the primary non-refoulement watch entities; any documented EU-facilitated return without individual assessment where a third-country national faces persecution constitutes a scored signal

EU AI Act GPAI transparency and systematic-risk documentation obligations take full effect August 2 — 57 days; non-compliance fines reach 35 million euros or 7% of global annual turnover; no assessed AI lab has published compliance documentation

The EU regulatory calendar is producing the highest density of scoring-trigger deadlines in any 60-day window in the current assessment cycle: June 12 (Returns Regulation) and August 2 (AI Act) bracket a 51-day window of structured scoring exposure across the Countries and AI Labs indexes

methodologyNotes.name: CODIFIED-IMPUNITY-ESCALATION (SCORABLE, BAND-CROSSING)
methodologyNotes.description: New ruling established this cycle for Bolivia; extends the June 5 STATE-REPRESSION-IN-FLUX ruling. The June 5 proposal priced attested lethal force and mass detention as un-priced harm on a non-floor Developing entity (−5.6, remained Developing). The June 6 evidence adds a codified impunity structure: President Paz signed a law authorizing military force against protesters with a presumption-of-legality shield deeming soldiers' actions lawful unless proven otherwise. Unlike incident-level casualties, a signed-into-law impunity shield is a durable state-conduct change that pre-commits the apparatus to non-acknowledgment of harm (AB1/AB2 floor-adjacent) and contradicts stated rights commitments (I4). This structural change pulls ACC to 1.3 and INT to 1.5 and — combined with the escalated attested toll (10 killed, 365 arrested) and near-full city blockades — crosses the entity into Critical (28.4→18.4, −10.0). The ruling supersedes the June 5 STATE-REPRESSION-IN-FLUX proposal at the larger magnitude. Confidence medium-high: terrorism charges against 25 union leaders remain filed-not-adjudicated; the magnitude rests on the signed law plus attested deaths and detentions, not charge outcomes.

methodologyNotes.name: PRICED-CHRONIC-REPRESSION + ACCOUNTABILITY-DOCUMENTATION-OF-PAST-HARM
methodologyNotes.description: New framing established this cycle for Venezuela. Where an entity's published score already encodes a decade-plus pattern of harm, and new evidence consists of external accountability findings documenting that same pattern rather than revealing new un-priced state conduct, the evidence confirms rather than lowers the score. Venezuela's published 18.0 Critical encodes GNB crimes against humanity, enforced disappearances, and political-prisoner detention at scale. The HRW universal-jurisdiction report and UN FFM crimes-against-humanity findings (December 2025) document this same pattern. Critically, the post-Maduro removal trajectory has produced a partial de-escalation (prisoner releases), which the FFM itself characterized as positive. Accountability findings against an entity become scorable only if they reveal new un-priced harm or an ICC warrant changes the gravity floor. Distinguish from: CODIFIED-IMPUNITY-ESCALATION (new structural conduct) and ACTIVE-PERPETRATION-FLOOR (absolute floor entities). Venezuela confirmed at 18.0.

methodologyNotes.name: NON-STATE-DRIVEN-DISPLACEMENT / PRICED-CONTINUATION
methodologyNotes.description: New framing established this cycle for Haiti. Where record displacement is driven predominantly by non-state actors (gang violence) amid a near-collapsed state, and the state entity's score already encodes profound incapacity rather than active-perpetration, quantitative deepening of that displacement does not produce a new scored proposal. Haiti's near-floor 4.7 encodes state incapacity across EQU, BND, ACC, SYS, and INT at the 1.0 minimum; the June record of 1.5 million displaced represents a deepening of a priced collapse rather than new state-attributable harm. Converts to a scored proposal — and potentially toward the absolute floor — only on documented state or MSS-force complicity in civilian harm.

methodologyNotes.name: SAME-REGIME-CONTINUATION / DUPLICATE-SUPPRESSED
methodologyNotes.description: Re-applied this cycle for the United States. The pending 06-04 proposal (23.4→17.5, Developing→Critical) already prices the conduct pattern. ICE's rescission of the 30-day post-release death-reporting requirement and the 18th in-custody death of 2026 are same-regime continuation that deepens the documented ACC 1.4/INT 1.5 floor without altering the −5.9 magnitude. No duplicate proposal was created; the June 6 evidence is logged as corroborating record for the open 06-04 proposal.

methodologyNotes.name: NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY-NON-DOUBLE-COUNT
methodologyNotes.description: Re-affirmed this cycle for Uganda (new application) and DRC (re-affirmed). The Ebola Bundibugyo PHEIC is an exogenous epidemiological shock revealing already-priced state-response capacity, absent documented deliberate state obstruction. Uganda (20.3, 0.3 points above Critical; Kampala cluster) and DRC (2.3, near-floor; 515/91, 20% contact tracing) both confirm at published scores. The conversion trigger for both entities is documented deliberate state interference with the WHO response: access denial, fund diversion, or case-data suppression. Kampala urban spread does not independently break the overlay; the conversion trigger is deliberate state conduct, not outbreak trajectory alone.

forwardTriggers.trigger: EU Returns Regulation enters force; documented EU-facilitated return without individual assessment where a third-country national faces persecution constitutes a scored signal under the RETURN-HUBS-AND-NON-REFOULEMENT anchor. India at 15.6 and Jordan at 35.9 are the primary non-refoulement watch entities.

WARN Act last-working-day window closes for approximately 30,000 affected workers. Adjudicated finding on the remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory in NJ state proceedings or the Washington class action constitutes an immediate scoring trigger from the 0.6-point-above-Critical baseline.

Ebola Bundibugyo peak window through July with confirmed Kampala cluster and 20% contact-tracing coverage. Documented deliberate state obstruction of the WHO response breaks the NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY hold at the near-floor 2.3 composite.

Phase-2 MDL deadline; merits adjudication or settlement of the Australia $1.43B PFAS claim or the Minnesota MPCA PFOS suit; ruling on military-contractor immunity defense. A modeled downgrade of −2.3 would cross from 0.3-points-above-Critical into Critical.

EU AI Act GPAI transparency and systematic-risk documentation obligations take full effect. Fines up to 35 million euros or 7% of global annual turnover. No assessed AI lab has published compliance documentation.

West and Central Africa lean season peak. Sudan and South Sudan sustain floor designation. Nigeria (21.9) at 1.9 points above Critical; further IPC Phase 5 area expansion or documented state obstruction of aid converts to a scorable downgrade.

Judicial annulment or legislative repeal of the June 8 presumption-of-legality military-force law is the primary reversal signal from the proposed 18.4 Critical baseline. Further military deployments with casualties under the law reinforce the proposal. Charge adjudication for the 25 union leaders is a secondary directional indicator.

Appellate review of Murphy (D. Mass.) and Cobb (D.D.C.) deportation injunctions. Reinstatement of the rescinded 30-day post-release death-reporting requirement would be the first upward-direction signal within the open 06-04 proposal.

Confirmation of 145-plus state-attributable enforced disappearances at scale beyond the priced refoulement pattern. Judicial findings on wrongful citizen expulsion also constitute a potential trigger for further scored movement from the applied 15.6 baseline.

ICC warrant issuance or a renewed mass-detention wave reversing the release trajectory — new scored event. Sustained and verified prisoner-release trajectory covering the documented 884 political prisoners, with judicial reform — first improvement signal from the 18.0 Critical baseline.

Merits adjudication or settlement of any of the three converging sovereign enforcement actions — DOJ MA-billing criminal probe, MA AG antitrust suit, AZ AG eight-insurer cartel suit — is the leading corporate scoring trigger of the current cycle.
DC Circuit ruling on Anthropic v. Hegseth (Pentagon supply-chain-risk designation). Favorable ruling triggers Functional/Established crossing from 59.1; adverse ruling produces downward movement.

dailyOpeningQuestion.text: If Bolivia's ACC and INT dimensional scores rest on the signed June 8 presumption-of-legality law as a codified impunity structure, does judicial annulment of that law — without any reduction in the attested death and detention toll — constitute sufficient evidence to reverse the dimensional moves that drove the Developing-to-Critical band crossing?
dailyOpeningQuestion.tensionFraming: The CODIFIED-IMPUNITY-ESCALATION ruling scores on the signed law as a durable state-conduct change — but the methodology has not yet specified whether judicial annulment of the law, absent toll reversal, is sufficient to reverse scores that also rest on attested deaths and detentions.
